name: Infection Burden (Broad Category)
definition: Infection burden is a broad exposure category reflecting frequency and severity of infectious exposures in a population (e.g., recurrent respiratory infections, endemic pathogen exposure). Infection can influence immune activation, inflammation, and tissue injury pathways relevant to multiple diseases. GENARCH includes this category to support educational gene–environment discussions without individual-level claims.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: Infections trigger innate and adaptive immune responses; chronic or repeated activation can alter inflammatory signaling in population contexts.

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: Early-life infections can shape immune maturation; interpretation depends strongly on pathogen, dose, and context.

references.title: Infection exposure and immune-mediated disease mechanisms: overview (review)
